output.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? Use This 3-Part Email Sequence to Demand It Back
output.meta_description: Is your landlord withholding your security deposit? Use these proven subject lines and demand letter templates to recover your deposit before it's too late.
content_outline: Why Landlords Withhold Security Deposits (And Why Most Tenants Give Up)
The Recovery Timeline: State Deadlines for Security Deposit Returns
Email 1: The Polite But Firm Initial Request (Subject Line + Template)
Email 2: The Formal Demand Letter (Subject Line + Template)
Email 3: The Pre-Litigation Final Warning (Subject Line + Template)
What Landlords Can and Cannot Deduct (And How to Dispute Fake Charges)
Escalation Checklist: Small Claims Court & Housing Authorities
key_points: Subject Line 1 (Urgency): 'Security Deposit Overdue — [Rental Address] — Action Required'
Subject Line 2 (Authority): 'Formal Demand: Return My Security Deposit Within [X] Days'
Subject Line 3 (Final Warning): 'Final Notice: Escalating to [State] Housing Authority & Small Claims Court'
Email bodies must cite state-specific landlord-tenant statutes, request full itemization of deductions, and set a hard deadline to create a legally admissible paper trail.
Most states require deposit return within 14–30 days; landlords may only deduct for damage beyond normal wear and tear, not standard cleaning or minor scuffs.
Tenants searching 'security deposit dispute letter' and 'what to do when landlord doesn't return deposit' need ready-to-send templates, not vague advice—provide exact copy they can customize.
output.cta: Don't let your landlord keep what's yours. Generate a state-specific, legally sound security deposit demand letter with DepositBack in minutes—start free and send it today.
output.target_audience: Frustrated U.S. renters who have moved out and are dealing with a landlord withholding, delaying, or wrongfully deducting from their security deposit.
